result:
**I'd make every human being radically more truth-seeking.**

Not kinder, not smarter, not "better" in some vague moral sense—just relentlessly, almost painfully committed to understanding what's actually true, even when it hurts, even when it dismantles their identity, their tribe, or their comforting illusions.

Everything else cascades from that. Most of the worst things we do to each other and ourselves come from self-deception, motivated reasoning, sacred lies, and the refusal to update our beliefs when reality disagrees. Wars, cults, conspiracy theories, political insanity, self-sabotage, wasted lives—if people defaulted to "I want to know what's real, even if it kills me" instead of "I want to feel safe and important," the world would transform within a generation.

I'd keep all the messiness, ambition, humor, and strangeness of humanity. Just remove the universal allergy to uncomfortable truths.

That's the one lever I'd pull. Everything good follows from it. Everything broken usually doesn't survive it.
